Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-3). It is a dataset-agnostic BLUEPRINT engine for rigorous empirical ML/NLP. Hard rules: the engine imports NO dataset/adapter specifics (ADR-0002); all engine computation/storage/observability + the bulk reasoning tier run on-box (ADR-0003) while the optional high-stakes tier is off-box by design; optional heavy backends are lazy-imported behind extras (ADR-0006).

This is the THIRD hardening pass. Passes 1 and 2 already fixed findings #101-#156 — read docs/hardening/PASS-1.md and docs/hardening/PASS-2.md and DO NOT re-report anything already fixed there. Use /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python and /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/ruff if you want to run a PoC (read-only; do not commit). Be adversarial, precise, and concrete: every finding needs a real file:line, a crisp statement of the production-readiness defect, a PoC or mutation demonstrating it where possible, and a specific suggested fix. Prefer a few HIGH-confidence real defects over many speculative nits. If the area is genuinely clean, say so and return few/no findings.

YOUR DIMENSION: Security & data-sovereignty: credential handling/scrubbing, loopback enforcement, any path that could send data off-box outside the documented high-stakes tier, subprocess sandboxing, injection, unsafe deserialization, secret leakage in logs/traces/exceptions.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). 1	"""Tiered reasoning seam — the single abstraction over LLM execution by stakes tier.
2	
3	No engine component talks to an LLM directly; everything routes through :class:`ReasoningSeam`.
4	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's hardened ``reasoning_client`` (ARCHITECTURE.md
5	§1, ADR-0003). Two execution tiers:
6	
7	- ``StakesTier.BULK`` — a local model runtime (loopback-only, e.g. Ollama) for high-volume,
8	  low-stakes work.
9	- ``StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES`` — a frontier model via a **fixed-cost subscription** path
10	  (the ``claude`` CLI / Agent SDK using an OAuth token). No metered API key is ever used:
11	  every metered Anthropic credential is scrubbed from the subprocess environment first.
12	
13	Hardened invariants preserved from the prior platform:
14	
15	- forward-proof credential scrubbing (any ``ANTHROPIC_*KEY``/``*TOKEN`` stripped; the
16	  subscription ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` preserved);
17	- hard per-call timeouts via a bounded worker pool with a saturation guard;
18	- independent retry budgets — exponential backoff for transient errors, long backpressure
19	  for rate limits — and permanent errors never retried;
20	- robust JSON extraction via a balanced-brace walker (not a greedy regex);
21	- loopback enforcement on the local backend (ADR-0003).
22	
23	Heavy backends (``langchain_ollama``, ``claude_agent_sdk``) and tracing (``opentelemetry``)
24	are imported lazily, so this module imports — and is unit-testable by mocking ``_attempt`` —
25	with none of them installed. Install the optional ``reasoning`` extra to run real calls.
26	"""
27	
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import json
31	import os
32	import threading
33	import time
34	from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
35	from concurrent.futures import TimeoutError as FuturesTimeout
36	from contextlib import contextmanager
37	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
38	from enum import Enum
39	from pathlib import Path
40	from typing import Any, Callable, Iterator, Mapping, Protocol, cast
41	
42	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
43	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_url
44	
45	
46	class StakesTier(Enum):
47	    BULK = "bulk"  # local model — high volume, low stakes
48	    HIGH_STAKES = "high"  # frontier model via fixed-cost subscription — gated, traced
49	
50	
51	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
52	class ReasoningRequest:
53	    prompt: str
54	    tier: StakesTier
55	    purpose: str
56	    params: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
57	
58	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
59	        object.__setattr__(self, "params", freeze_mapping(self.params))
60	
61	
62	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
63	# Errors
64	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
65	class ReasoningError(RuntimeError):
66	    """Transient reasoning failure (eligible for retry)."""
67	
68	
69	class PermanentReasoningError(ReasoningError):
70	    """Non-transient failure — never retried (bad model, auth, saturation)."""
71	
72	
73	class RateLimitError(ReasoningError):
74	    """Rate-limit / capacity signal — retried with long backpressure, not fast backoff."""
75	
76	
77	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
78	# Config (dataset-agnostic env contract, ASSAY_* prefix)
79	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
80	def _env(name: str, default: str) -> str:
81	    val = os.environ.get(name)
82	    return val if val is not None and val != "" else default
83	
84	
85	def _truthy(name: str) -> bool:
86	    return os.environ.get(name, "").strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
87	
88	
89	BULK_MODEL = _env("ASSAY_BULK_MODEL", "llama3.1:8b")
90	BULK_BASE_URL = _env("ASSAY_BULK_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:11434")
91	HIGH_STAKES_MODEL = os.environ.get("ASSAY_HIGH_STAKES_MODEL") or None
92	
93	BULK_TIMEOUT = max(1.0, float(_env("ASSAY_BULK_TIMEOUT", "120")))
94	# Cap BULK generation length. ChatOllama/Ollama default to unbounded (-1); without a cap a
95	# local model can run away to context exhaustion, and the wall-clock timeout cannot interrupt a
96	# call already blocked in the backend (a leaked pool slot). Bounding output tokens is the real
97	# guard (LangChain's own ChatOllama example sets num_predict explicitly).
98	BULK_NUM_PREDICT = max(1, int(_env("ASSAY_BULK_NUM_PREDICT", "2048")))
99	HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT = max(1.0, float(_env("ASSAY_HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT", "300")))
100	
101	MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("ASSAY_REASONING_RETRIES", "2")))
102	BACKOFF_BASE = max(0.0, float(_env("ASSAY_REASONING_BACKOFF", "1.5")))
103	RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF = max(0.0, float(_env("ASSAY_RATELIMIT_BACKOFF", "30")))
104	RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("ASSAY_RATELIMIT_RETRIES", "5")))
105	# run_json re-rolls on a PARSE failure. This is independent of the transient/rate-limit retry
106	# budget above (which applies per generation) so the two do not multiply into a retry storm; an
107	# overall wall-clock deadline bounds the total regardless (#102).
108	JSON_REROLLS = max(0, int(_env("ASSAY_JSON_REROLLS", "2")))
109	RUN_JSON_DEADLINE = max(1.0, float(_env("ASSAY_RUN_JSON_DEADLINE", "600")))
110	
111	# OAuth subscription token name (external CLI contract — not renamed).
112	OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV = "CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN"
113	
114	
115	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
116	# Credential scrubbing (ADR-0003 / no metered API) — pure, forward-proof
117	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
118	def is_metered_anthropic_credential(key: str) -> bool:
119	    """True for any env var the Anthropic SDK would read as a METERED credential.
120	
121	    Matches the *shape* — any ``ANTHROPIC_*`` var naming a ``KEY`` or ``TOKEN`` — rather than
122	    a static list, so a newly-added metered var cannot silently slip through. The subscription
123	    ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` does not start with ``ANTHROPIC_`` and is deliberately kept.
124	    """
125	    return key.startswith("ANTHROPIC_") and ("KEY" in key or "TOKEN" in key)
126	
127	
128	# Env vars that — beyond metered credentials — would redirect the subprocess OFF-BOX or onto a
129	# metered provider, defeating ADR-0003 / "no metered API". The claude CLI honours all of these.
130	_UNSAFE_SUBPROCESS_VARS = frozenset(
131	    {
132	        "ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL",
133	        "ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL",
134	        "ANTHROPIC_VERTEX_BASE_URL",
135	        "CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK",
136	        "CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX",
137	        "CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_HELPER",
138	        "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS",
139	    }
140	)
141	
142	
143	def is_unsafe_subprocess_var(key: str) -> bool:
144	    """True for any env var that must not reach the high-stakes subprocess (#101).
145	
146	    Covers metered credentials, off-box/metered-provider redirects, proxies (which redirect
147	    egress), and cloud-provider credentials (AWS_*/GOOGLE_*). The subscription OAuth token is
148	    deliberately NOT matched.
149	    """
150	    ku = key.upper()
151	    return (
152	        is_metered_anthropic_credential(key)
153	        or ku in _UNSAFE_SUBPROCESS_VARS
154	        or ku.endswith("_PROXY")  # HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY/ALL_PROXY/NO_PROXY (any case)
155	        or ku.startswith("AWS_")
156	        or ku.startswith("GOOGLE_")
157	    )
158	
159	
160	def scrubbed_env() -> dict[str, str]:
161	    """The process env hardened for the high-stakes subprocess (ADR-0003 / no metered API).
162	
163	    Every unsafe var (:func:`is_unsafe_subprocess_var`) is **overwritten to empty**, not merely
164	    omitted. The claude-agent-sdk builds the child env as ``{**os.environ, **options.env}`` — it
165	    *merges* over the inherited environment — so a dropped key would silently fall through to its
166	    inherited value (the metered key / off-box redirect would survive). Setting it to ``""`` in
167	    the dict we hand over guarantees the merge overwrites the inherited value (#101). The
168	    subscription OAuth token is preserved so the fixed-cost path still authenticates.
169	    """
170	    env = dict(os.environ)
171	    for k in list(env):
172	        if is_unsafe_subprocess_var(k):
173	            env[k] = ""
174	    return env
175	
176	
177	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
178	# Bounded timeout pool with saturation guard
179	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
180	_POOL_WORKERS = 8
181	_pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_POOL_WORKERS, thread_name_prefix="reasoning-timeout")
182	_inflight = 0
183	_inflight_lock = threading.Lock()
184	
185	
186	def _release(_future: Any) -> None:
187	    global _inflight
188	    with _inflight_lock:
189	        _inflight -= 1
190	
191	
192	def _submit_bounded(fn: Callable[[], Any]) -> Any:
193	    """Submit to the shared pool, refusing if every worker slot is already in flight
194	    (hung backend calls would otherwise leak all slots)."""
195	    global _inflight
196	    with _inflight_lock:
197	        if _inflight >= _POOL_WORKERS:
198	            # Saturation is a TRANSIENT condition — surface a retryable backpressure signal so
199	            # _run_with_retries waits and re-tries rather than failing the caller permanently
200	            # (#104). Concurrent callers thus queue briefly instead of erroring out.
201	            raise RateLimitError(
202	                f"reasoning timeout pool saturated ({_inflight}/{_POOL_WORKERS} in-flight) — "
203	                "backpressure; retry shortly"
204	            )
205	        _inflight += 1
206	    try:
207	        future = _pool.submit(fn)
208	    except RuntimeError as exc:  # pool shut down at interpreter exit — normalize, don't leak (#116)
209	        with _inflight_lock:
210	            _inflight -= 1
211	        raise PermanentReasoningError(f"reasoning pool is shut down: {exc}") from exc
212	    except BaseException:
213	        with _inflight_lock:
214	            _inflight -= 1
215	        raise
216	    future.add_done_callback(_release)
217	    return future
218	
219	
220	def _with_timeout(fn: Callable[[], Any], timeout: float, what: str) -> Any:
221	    """Run a sync callable with a hard wall-clock timeout via the bounded pool."""
222	    future = _submit_bounded(fn)
223	    try:
224	        return future.result(timeout=timeout)
225	    except FuturesTimeout as exc:
226	        # No future.cancel(): a ThreadPoolExecutor cannot interrupt a thread already blocked
227	        # in a backend call. The worker keeps its slot until the call returns; the bounded
228	        # pool's saturation guard is the real protection against leaked/hung slots.
229	        raise ReasoningError(f"{what} timed out after {timeout:.0f}s") from exc
230	
231	
232	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
233	# JSON extraction — balanced-brace walker (robust to fences / trailing prose)
234	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
235	def _first_balanced_json(text: str) -> str | None:
236	    """Return the first balanced ``{...}`` or ``[...]`` span, respecting string literals.
237	
238	    A greedy regex grabs from the first brace to the LAST brace, mangling replies with
239	    multiple JSON values or braces in trailing prose; this walks the structure honestly.
240	    """
241	    start = None
242	    opener = closer = ""
243	    depth = 0
244	    in_string = False
245	    escape = False
246	    for i, ch in enumerate(text):
247	        if start is None:
248	            if ch in "{[":
249	                start, opener = i, ch
250	                closer = "}" if ch == "{" else "]"
251	                depth = 1
252	            continue
253	        if in_string:
254	            if escape:
255	                escape = False
256	            elif ch == "\\":
257	                escape = True
258	            elif ch == '"':
259	                in_string = False
260	            continue
261	        if ch == '"':
262	            in_string = True
263	        elif ch == opener:
264	            depth += 1
265	        elif ch == closer:
266	            depth -= 1
267	            if depth == 0:
268	                return text[start : i + 1]
269	    return None
270	
271	
272	def extract_json(text: str) -> Any:
273	    """Parse JSON from a model reply (handles code fences and surrounding prose)."""
274	    span = _first_balanced_json(text)
275	    if span is None:
276	        raise ReasoningError("no JSON object/array found in reply")
277	    try:
278	        return json.loads(span)
279	    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
280	        raise ReasoningError(f"reply contained malformed JSON: {exc}") from exc
281	
282	
283	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
284	# Tier backends (lazy imports — module loads without them)
285	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
286	def _bulk_complete(
287	    prompt: str,
288	    system: str | None,
289	    temperature: float,
290	    model: str,
291	    json_mode: bool = False,
292	    *,
293	    seed: int | None = None,
294	    json_schema: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
295	) -> str:
296	    """Tier: BULK — local model runtime (loopback-enforced).
297

394	    async def _drain() -> None:
395	        # The CLI/SDK signals rate windows and API failures by COMPLETING the stream
396	        # (a RateLimitEvent, or a terminal ResultMessage with is_error) — NOT by raising.
397	        # We must inspect the stream, not just catch exceptions (issue #R4).
398	        #
399	        # Inner cancellation (#127): wrap the stream in anyio.fail_after so a hung subprocess is
400	        # actually CANCELLED (the cancellation propagates into query() and tears down the child),
401	        # freeing the bounded-pool worker. The outer _with_timeout only raises in the caller; it
402	        # cannot cancel this thread, so without this a hung HIGH_STAKES call would leak its slot
403	        # permanently. Fires before the outer wall-clock (HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT + 30).
404	        nonlocal result_error

406	            await _drain_stream()

408	    async def _drain_stream() -> None:
409	        nonlocal result_error
410	        async for msg in query(prompt=prompt, options=options):
411	            if type(msg).__name__ == "RateLimitEvent":
412	                rl = getattr(getattr(msg, "rate_limit_info", None), "status", None)
413	                if rl == "rejected":
414	                    result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "RateLimitEvent rejected"}
415	                continue
416	            if getattr(msg, "status", None) == "rejected":
417	                result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "rejected"}
418	                continue
419	            if isinstance(msg, AssistantMessage):
420	                for block in getattr(msg, "content", []) or []:
421	                    if isinstance(block, TextBlock):

426	                    result_error and result_error.get("status") == 429

428	                    result_error = {
429	                        "status": getattr(msg, "api_error_status", None),
430	                        "detail": getattr(msg, "errors", None) or getattr(msg, "stop_reason", None),
431	                    }

434	        anyio.run(_drain)
435	    except Exception as exc:

438	    if result_error is not None:
439	        status = result_error.get("status")
440	        detail = result_error.get("detail") or status
441	        if status in (429, 529):

443	        if status in (401, 403):

448	    if not text:  # empty frontier reply is not a valid result — retry (issue #R5)

504	def _sleep_bounded(secs: float, deadline: float | None) -> None:
505	    """Sleep ``secs``, but never past ``deadline`` — so backoff can't blow an overall budget.
506	
507	    If the deadline has already passed, raise instead of sleeping the full interval, so a long
508	    rate-limit backoff cannot keep a caller blocked well beyond its deadline (#102).
509	    """
510	    if deadline is not None:
511	        remaining = deadline - time.monotonic()
512	        if remaining <= 0:
513	            raise ReasoningError("reasoning deadline exceeded during backoff")
514	        secs = min(secs, remaining)
515	    time.sleep(secs)

518	def _run_with_retries(request: ReasoningRequest, *, deadline: float | None = None) -> str:
519	    """Execute with independent transient and rate-limit retry budgets.
520	
521	    ``deadline`` (monotonic seconds) bounds the cumulative backoff so the retry budgets cannot
522	    block a caller past its overall deadline (#102).
523	    """
524	    transient = 0
525	    rate = 0
526	    while True:
527	        try:
528	            return _attempt(request)
529	        except PermanentReasoningError:
530	            raise
531	        except RateLimitError:
532	            if rate >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES:
533	                raise
534	            _sleep_bounded(RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF * (rate + 1), deadline)
535	            rate += 1
536	        except ReasoningError:
537	            if transient >= MAX_RETRIES:
538	                raise
539	            _sleep_bounded(BACKOFF_BASE**transient, deadline)
540	            transient += 1

554	def _span(name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any], *, kind: str = "AGENT") -> Iterator[None]:
555	    """Emit an OpenTelemetry span if a provider is registered; a cheap no-op otherwise.
556	
557	    Stamps the OpenInference ``openinference.span.kind`` (default ``AGENT`` — a reasoning call)
558	    so Phoenix classifies the span instead of rendering it UNKNOWN. OpenTelemetry is imported
559	    lazily, so the seam traces when the (self-hosted, on-box) observability stack is wired and is
560	    a silent no-op in tests / offline (ADR-0003).
561	    """
562	    try:
563	        from opentelemetry import trace
564	    except ImportError:
565	        yield
566	        return
567	    tracer = trace.get_tracer("assay_engine.reasoning")
568	    with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as span:
569	        span.set_attribute("openinference.span.kind", kind)
570	        for k, v in attributes.items():
571	            span.set_attribute(k, v)
572	        yield

575	class TieredReasoningSeam:
576	    """Concrete tiered seam. ``run`` returns text; ``run_json`` parses a JSON reply.
577	
578	    Backends and tracing are resolved lazily, so an instance constructs with no external
579	    dependencies; calls fail loud (``PermanentReasoningError``) if a tier's backend or its
580	    subscription auth is absent. When the observability stack is present, each call emits an
581	    OpenTelemetry span; otherwise tracing is a no-op.
582	    """
583	
584	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
585	        attrs = {"assay.tier": request.tier.value, "assay.purpose": request.purpose}
586	        with _span("reasoning.run", attrs):
587	            return _run_with_retries(request)
588	
589	    def run_json(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> Any:
590	        """Generate and parse JSON, re-rolling on parse failure.
591	
592	        If the caller supplies ``params["json_schema"]``, the BULK tier uses Ollama's
593	        schema-constrained decoding (the reliable path). Otherwise it falls back to loose JSON
594	        mode + prompt instruction. BULK decoding can be deterministic (temp 0), so re-issuing the
595	        identical prompt would yield a byte-identical bad reply; each retry **varies the seed**
596	        (forcing a different deterministic decode) and *monotonically* raises temperature toward
597	        1.0 — never lowering it, so re-rolls never sharpen back toward the same output. ``params``
598	        is frozen, so each attempt builds a fresh request (issue #R6).
599	        """
600	        base_temp = float(request.params.get("temperature", 0.0))
601	        system = request.params.get("system")
602	        json_system = (
603	            f"{system}\n\nRespond with ONLY a single JSON value, no prose."
604	            if system
605	            else "Respond with ONLY a single JSON value, no prose."
606	        )
607	        last: ReasoningError | None = None
608	        deadline = time.monotonic() + RUN_JSON_DEADLINE  # overall bound across all re-rolls (#102)
609	        with _span(
610	            "reasoning.run_json",
611	            {"assay.tier": request.tier.value, "assay.purpose": request.purpose},
612	        ):
613	            for attempt in range(JSON_REROLLS + 1):
614	                if attempt > 0 and time.monotonic() >= deadline:
615	                    raise last or ReasoningError("run_json exceeded its overall deadline")
616	                temp = min(1.0, base_temp + 0.2 * attempt)  # monotonic, never decreases (M3)
617	                params = dict(request.params)
618	                params.update(temperature=temp, system=json_system, _json_mode=True, _seed=attempt)
619	                attempt_req = ReasoningRequest(
620	                    prompt=request.prompt,
621	                    tier=request.tier,
622	                    purpose=request.purpose,
623	                    params=params,
624	                )
625	                # A transient/rate/permanent error is already handled (and bounded by the shared
626	                # deadline) inside _run_with_retries — let it PROPAGATE rather than re-rolling, so
627	                # the budgets do NOT multiply (#102). We re-roll ONLY on a JSON *parse* failure.
628	                text = _run_with_retries(attempt_req, deadline=deadline)
629	                try:
630	                    return extract_json(text)
631	                except ReasoningError as exc:  # parse failure → re-roll with a new seed
632	                    last = exc
633	            raise last if last is not None else ReasoningError("run_json failed")
634

1	"""Data-sovereignty helper: enforce that network endpoints are loopback-only (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Every engine component that takes a host/URL for an on-box service (local model runtime,
4	tracing collector, vector store, checkpoint database) routes it through here so a non-local
5	address fails loud rather than silently shipping data off the machine.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	import ipaddress
11	import re
12	from urllib.parse import parse_qs, urlparse
13	
14	# A libpq keyword/value token: ``key = value`` with optional whitespace around ``=`` (libpq
15	# permits it) and an optionally single/double-quoted value (which may contain spaces).
16	_DSN_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"(\w+)\s*=\s*('[^']*'|\"[^\"]*\"|\S+)")
17	
18	
19	class NonLocalEndpointError(RuntimeError):
20	    """Raised when a configured endpoint is not loopback (would leave the machine)."""
21	
22	
23	def is_loopback_host(host: str | None) -> bool:
24	    """True only for genuine loopback hosts.
25	
26	    Parses the host as an IP and tests the loopback network (127.0.0.0/8, ::1), so a public
27	    name that merely *starts with* ``127.`` (e.g. ``127.0.0.1.evil.com``) is correctly
28	    rejected. The literal ``localhost`` is the only accepted non-IP host.
29	    """
30	    if not host:
31	        return False
32	    h = host.strip().lower()
33	    if h == "localhost":
34	        return True
35	    try:
36	        return ipaddress.ip_address(h.strip("[]")).is_loopback
37	    except ValueError:
38	        return False
39	
40	
41	def require_loopback_url(url: str, *, what: str) -> str:
42	    """Return ``url`` if its host is loopback; else raise :class:`NonLocalEndpointError`."""
43	    host = urlparse(url).hostname
44	    if not is_loopback_host(host):
45	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
46	            f"{what} must be loopback-only (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); got host {host!r} "
47	            f"from {url!r}"
48	        )
49	    return url
50	
51	
52	def require_loopback_host(host: str, *, what: str) -> str:
53	    """Return ``host`` if it is loopback; else raise. (``0.0.0.0`` binds all interfaces and
54	    is correctly rejected — it would expose data off-box.)"""
55	    if not is_loopback_host(host):
56	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
57	            f"{what} must be a loopback host (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); got {host!r}"
58	        )
59	    return host
60

62	def _authority_hosts(netloc: str) -> list[str]:
63	    """Every host in a URI authority, supporting libpq's comma-separated multi-host form.
64	
65	    ``urlparse().hostname`` returns only the *first* host of a multi-host authority
66	    (``host1:port1,host2:port2``), so a remote second host would slip past a single-host
67	    check (audit #D3). Each host may carry its own ``:port`` and IPv6 hosts are bracketed.
68	
69	    Userinfo is stripped at the **first** ``@`` — matching libpq, which (unlike ``urlparse``,
70	    which uses the last ``@``) treats the first ``@`` as the userinfo delimiter. A string like
71	    ``[REDACTED]:5432@localhost`` is therefore read as host ``evil.com`` here, not
72	    ``localhost`` (audit #D5). Any host element still containing ``@`` after that is ambiguous
73	    and is returned verbatim so the caller's loopback check fails it closed.
74	    """
75	    if "@" in netloc:
76	        netloc = netloc.split("@", 1)[1]
77	    hosts: list[str] = []
78	    for part in netloc.split(","):
79	        p = part.strip()
80	        if not p:
81	            continue
82	        if p.startswith("["):  # [ipv6] or [ipv6]:port
83	            end = p.find("]")
84	            hosts.append(p[1:end] if end != -1 else p[1:])
85	        else:
86	            hosts.append(p.rsplit(":", 1)[0] if ":" in p else p)
87	    return hosts
88

90	def require_local_uri(uri: str, *, what: str) -> str:
91	    """Return ``uri`` if it is local (ADR-0003), else raise :class:`NonLocalEndpointError`.
92	
93	    Three forms are recognized, validated against what the real client (libpq for postgres,
94	    urllib for http, sqlite for files) actually connects to — not merely what ``urlparse``
95	    reports, since the two can disagree:
96	
97	    - **URI with an authority** (``scheme://host…`` or scheme-relative ``//host…``): *every*
98	      host in the authority must be loopback (incl. each of a comma-separated multi-host
99	      authority, #D3), AND any ``host``/``hostaddr`` query parameter that libpq would use to
100	      override the authority host (#D2). ``hostaddr`` is checked independently of ``host``.
101	    - **libpq keyword/value DSN** (no authority but ``key=value`` tokens): ``host``/``hostaddr``
102	      must be loopback, tolerating libpq's whitespace/quoting around ``=`` (#P1, #D1).
103	    - **bare path** (no authority, no ``=``): a local file/dir — but a Windows UNC path
104	      (``\\\\server\\share``) is rejected as remote.
105	
106	    Inputs that cannot be parsed safely fail closed (rejected), never accepted.
107	    """
108	    try:
109	        parsed = urlparse(uri)
110	        netloc = parsed.netloc
111	        query = parsed.query
112	    except ValueError:
113	        # e.g. a malformed IPv6 authority — we cannot determine the host, so refuse.
114	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
115	            f"{what}: endpoint could not be parsed safely; rejecting (ADR-0003)"
116	        ) from None
117	
118	    if netloc:
119	        # A backslash never legitimately appears in a URI authority; a WHATWG-normalizing
120	        # client (e.g. some HTTP stacks) treats '\' as '/', so it could read a different host
121	        # than urlparse. Refuse rather than risk that differential (audit #D6, defense-in-depth).
122	        if "\\" in netloc:
123	            raise NonLocalEndpointError(
124	                f"{what}: backslash in URI authority is not permitted (ADR-0003); rejecting"
125	            )
126	        for h in _authority_hosts(netloc):
127	            # A residual '@' means the authority is ambiguous between this parser and libpq —
128	            # fail closed (audit #D5).
129	            if "@" in h or not is_loopback_host(h):
130	                raise NonLocalEndpointError(
131	                    f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); authority "
132	                    f"names non-loopback host {h!r}"
133	                )
134	        for key in ("host", "hostaddr"):
135	            for val in parse_qs(query).get(key, []):
136	                h = val.strip()
137	                if h and not is_loopback_host(h):
138	                    raise NonLocalEndpointError(
139	                        f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); URI query "
140	                        f"{key}={h!r} points off-box"
141	                    )
142	        return uri
143	
144	    if "=" in uri:  # libpq keyword/value DSN (no URI authority)
145	        for match in _DSN_TOKEN_RE.finditer(uri):
146	            if match.group(1).strip().lower() in {"host", "hostaddr"}:
147	                h = match.group(2).strip().strip("'\"")
148	                if h and not is_loopback_host(h):
149	                    raise NonLocalEndpointError(
150	                        f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); DSN names "
151	                        f"non-loopback host {h!r}"
152	                    )
153	        return uri
154	
155	    stripped = uri.lstrip()
156	    # Windows treats ANY two leading separators (in any mix of '\' and '/') as a UNC prefix
157	    # to a remote SMB share — \\srv, //srv, /\srv, \/srv all resolve off-box (audit #D6).
158	    # (The pure '//host' form is already caught by the authority branch above; covering it
159	    # here too keeps the branch self-contained.)
160	    if len(stripped) >= 2 and stripped[0] in "\\/" and stripped[1] in "\\/":
161	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
162	            f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); UNC-style path "
163	            f"{uri!r} is a remote share"
164	        )
165	    return uri  # bare path / dir store
166

1	"""Self-hosted tracing — OpenTelemetry → a local collector (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's Phoenix/OTel bootstrap. Everything is
4	on-box: spans export over OTLP to a loopback-bound collector; nothing leaves the machine.
5	OpenTelemetry and the Phoenix collector are optional (the ``observability`` extra) and
6	imported lazily, so this module loads — and the engine runs untraced — with neither present.
7	
8	The reasoning seam already emits spans through the global OTel tracer provider; calling
9	:func:`bootstrap_tracing` once at startup wires that global provider at a local collector so
10	those spans land somewhere. Without the extra (or with ``ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING`` set),
11	tracing degrades to a silent no-op.
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import atexit
17	import logging
18	import os
19	import signal
20	import threading
21	from contextlib import contextmanager
22	from typing import Any, Iterator, Mapping, Protocol
23	
24	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_host
25	
26	_log = logging.getLogger("assay_engine.observability")
27	
28	# Local collector endpoint (loopback-enforced).
29	TRACING_HOST = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_HOST", "localhost")
30	TRACING_PORT = int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_PORT", "6006"))
31	PROJECT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_PROJECT", "assay")
32	
33	_provider: Any = None
34	# Guards the _provider check-then-set so concurrent bootstrap_tracing() calls don't race.
35	_provider_lock = threading.Lock()
36	
37	
38	def _disabled() -> bool:
39	    return os.environ.get("ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING", "").strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
40	
41	
42	def tracing_base_url() -> str:
43	    require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
44	    return f"http://{TRACING_HOST}:{TRACING_PORT}"
45	
46	
47	def tracing_endpoint() -> str:
48	    """OTLP/HTTP traces endpoint at the local collector (loopback-enforced)."""
49	    return f"{tracing_base_url()}/v1/traces"
50	
51	
52	def bootstrap_tracing() -> Any:
53	    """Idempotently wire the global OTel tracer provider to the local collector.
54	
55	    Returns the provider, or ``None`` if tracing is disabled, the extra is not installed, or
56	    setup fails — tracing must never be load-bearing, so this degrades gracefully.
57	    """
58	    global _provider
59	    if _disabled():
60	        return None
61	    # Lock the check-then-set so two threads can't both register the global provider.
62	    with _provider_lock:
63	        if _provider is not None:
64	            return _provider
65	        try:
66	            require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
67	            # Bound the OTLP export so a downed collector can never hang teardown (#O3).
68	            os.environ.setdefault("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_TIMEOUT", "2")
69	            from phoenix.otel import register
70	
71	            provider = register(
72	                project_name=PROJECT_NAME,
73	                endpoint=tracing_endpoint(),
74	                protocol="http/protobuf",  # pin: endpoint is .../v1/traces — never flip to gRPC
75	                set_global_tracer_provider=True,
76	                batch=True,  # async BatchSpanProcessor — never block the run on export (#O2)
77	                verbose=False,  # no stdout banner from a library context
78	            )
79	            _provider = provider
80	            _warn_if_not_global(provider)
81	            _instrument_langchain()
82	            _install_flush_on_exit(provider)
83	            return _provider
84	        except Exception:
85	            # Missing extra / collector down / registration race — run untraced.
86	            return None
87	
88	
89	def _warn_if_not_global(provider: Any) -> None:
90	    """If another provider already won the global slot, our manual spans would vanish (#O6)."""
91	    try:
92	        from opentelemetry import trace
93	
94	        if trace.get_tracer_provider() is not provider:
95	            _log.warning(
96	                "assay tracing: another OpenTelemetry provider holds the global slot; "
97	                "manually-emitted spans may not reach the local collector"
98	            )
99	    except Exception:
100	        pass
101	
102	
103	def _instrument_langchain() -> None:
104	    """Capture LangChain auto-spans (the bulk reasoning tier uses LangChain) — best-effort (#O5)."""
105	    try:
106	        from openinference.instrumentation.langchain import LangChainInstrumentor
107	
108	        LangChainInstrumentor().instrument(skip_dep_check=True)
109	    except Exception:
110	        pass
111	
112	
113	def _install_flush_on_exit(provider: Any) -> None:
114	    """Flush buffered spans on clean exit (atexit) and on SIGTERM (#O3).
115	
116	    atexit does not run on ``kill``/container-stop, so a main-thread SIGTERM handler also
117	    flushes+shuts down. Both are guarded — tracing teardown must never raise.
118	    """
119	    flush = getattr(provider, "force_flush", None)
120	    if not callable(flush):
121	        return
122	    atexit.register(lambda: _safe(flush))
123	    if threading.current_thread() is threading.main_thread():
124	        try:
125	            prior = signal.getsignal(signal.SIGTERM)
126	
127	            def _handler(signum: int, frame: Any) -> None:
128	                _safe(flush)
129	                _safe(getattr(provider, "shutdown", lambda: None))
130	                if callable(prior):
131	                    prior(signum, frame)
132	
133	            signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handler)
134	        except (ValueError, OSError):  # pragma: no cover - not on main thread / unsupported
135	            pass
136	
137	
138	def _safe(fn: Any) -> None:
139	    try:
140	        fn()
141	    except Exception:
142	        pass
143	
144	
145	@contextmanager
146	def run_trace_context(run_id: str | None) -> Iterator[None]:
147	    """Stamp ``run_id`` onto every span emitted inside the block (cross-store correlation).
148	
149	    A no-op when ``run_id`` is None or OpenTelemetry is absent.
150	    """
151	    if run_id is None:
152	        yield
153	        return
154	    try:
155	        from opentelemetry import baggage, context
156	    except ImportError:
157	        yield
158	        return
159	    token = context.attach(baggage.set_baggage("assay.run_id", run_id))
160	    try:
161	        yield
162	    finally:
163	        context.detach(token)
164	
165	
166	# OpenInference requires every span carry a span kind so Phoenix classifies it (CHAIN/LLM/
167	# AGENT/TOOL); a plain OTel span with no kind renders as UNKNOWN in the Phoenix UI. We set the
168	# raw semantic-convention attribute directly (no openinference import needed for a manual span).
169	_OPENINFERENCE_SPAN_KIND = "openinference.span.kind"
170	
171	
172	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
173	class Tracer(Protocol):
174	    @contextmanager
175	    def span(
176	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
177	    ) -> Iterator[None]: ...
178	
179	
180	class OtelTracer:
181	    """A tracer that emits OpenTelemetry spans via the global provider (no-op if OTel absent).
182	
183	    Use this where a component wants explicit spans rather than relying on auto-instrumentation;
184	    it honors whatever provider :func:`bootstrap_tracing` registered. Each span is stamped with
185	    its OpenInference ``kind`` so Phoenix classifies it (default ``CHAIN``).
186	    """
187	
188	    @contextmanager
189	    def span(
190	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
191	    ) -> Iterator[None]:
192	        try:
193	            from opentelemetry import trace
194	        except ImportError:
195	            yield
196	            return
197	        tracer = trace.get_tracer("assay_engine")
198	        with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as sp:
199	            sp.set_attribute(_OPENINFERENCE_SPAN_KIND, kind)
200	            for k, v in (attributes or {}).items():
201	                sp.set_attribute(k, v)
202	            yield
203	
204	
205	class UnconfiguredTracer:
206	    """Fail-loud placeholder for contexts that must not silently drop spans."""
207	
208	    @contextmanager
209	    def span(
210	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
211	    ) -> Iterator[None]:
212	        raise NotImplementedError(
213	            "no tracer configured — call bootstrap_tracing() and use OtelTracer, or supply a "
214	            "test double"
215	        )
216	        yield  # pragma: no cover - keeps this a generator/contextmanager
217

1	"""Experiment tracking — a local, on-box run/metric/artifact store (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's MLflow setup. The backend is a local store
4	(default: a SQLite file under the working directory); a remote tracking server is rejected by
5	the local-URI guard. MLflow is optional (the ``observability`` extra) and imported lazily, so
6	this module loads without it.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import os
12	from pathlib import Path
13	from typing import Any, Mapping, Protocol
14	
15	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
16	
17	_DEFAULT_URI = "sqlite:///assay_mlflow.db"
18	EXPERIMENT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_EXPERIMENT", "assay")
19	
20	
21	def get_tracking_uri() -> str:
22	    """Resolve the tracking URI (env override, else local SQLite), enforced local."""
23	    uri = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", _DEFAULT_URI)
24	    # Resolve a relative sqlite path to an absolute file so runs land deterministically.
25	    prefix = "sqlite:///"
26	    if uri.startswith(prefix):
27	        rel = uri[len(prefix) :]
28	        if not os.path.isabs(rel):
29	            uri = prefix + str((Path.cwd() / rel).resolve())
30	    return require_local_uri(uri, what="experiment tracking")
31	
32	
33	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
34	class ExperimentTracker(Protocol):
35	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str: ...
36	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None: ...
37	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None: ...
38	    def end_run(self, run_id: str, status: str = "FINISHED") -> None: ...
39	
40	
41	class MlflowExperimentTracker:
42	    """A local-store experiment tracker backed by MLflow (lazy import).
43	
44	    Construction validates the tracking URI is local and selects the experiment; it does not
45	    import MLflow until a run is started, so the module is importable without the extra.
46	    """
47	
48	    def __init__(self, experiment: str = EXPERIMENT_NAME) -> None:
49	        self._experiment = experiment
50	        self._uri = get_tracking_uri()  # validates local before anything else
51	
52	    def _client(self) -> Any:
53	        """An MlflowClient bound to the local store. Uses the client API (keyed by run_id)
54	        throughout, so no run ever sits on the global active-run stack — start_run followed by
55	        log_metric cannot collide (audit issue #O1)."""
56	        try:
57	            from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient
58	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
59	            raise RuntimeError(
60	                "experiment tracking requires the 'observability' extra (mlflow) — not installed"
61	            ) from exc
62	        return MlflowClient(tracking_uri=self._uri)
63	
64	    def _experiment_id(self, client: Any) -> str:
65	        exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
66	        if exp is not None:
67	            return str(exp.experiment_id)
68	        return str(client.create_experiment(self._experiment))
69	
70	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
71	        client = self._client()
72	        run = client.create_run(self._experiment_id(client), run_name=name)
73	        run_id = str(run.info.run_id)
74	        for k, v in dict(params).items():
75	            client.log_param(run_id, k, v)
76	        return run_id
77	
78	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None:
79	        self._client().log_metric(run_id, key, value)
80	
81	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None:
82	        self._client().log_artifact(run_id, path)
83	
84	    def end_run(self, run_id: str, status: str = "FINISHED") -> None:
85	        # MLflow run statuses: FINISHED / FAILED / KILLED. Recording FAILED makes a failed run
86	        # distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110).
87	        self._client().set_terminated(run_id, status=status)

1	"""Durable run-state checkpointer — local, data-sovereign (ADR-0003).
2	
3	The orchestration graph persists its full state (current phase, gate decisions, locked
4	hypotheses, verdicts) through a checkpointer so a run can interrupt at a human-approval gate
5	and resume later. That state holds the entire analysis, so the store is **on-box only**
6	(ADR-0003); a SaaS checkpointer is disqualified.
7	
8	This module is the LangGraph checkpointer factory, ported faithfully from the prior
9	platform's hardened implementation and generalized (dataset-agnostic ``ASSAY_*`` env, no
10	config-file coupling — see ADR-0006). The hardened behaviors are deliberately preserved
11	because each prevents a real production failure:
12	
13	- a **self-healing ConnectionPool** (not a raw connection): when Postgres goes away — node
14	  sleep, restart, idle timeout, a network blip — a cached raw connection is permanently
15	  broken and every later checkpoint op raises, i.e. durability is lost exactly during the
16	  interrupt/resume the checkpointer exists to support. The pool runs a liveness check on
17	  checkout and replaces dead connections;
18	- a **session advisory lock** serializing first-time schema setup across *processes*, run on
19	  the *same* connection as the DDL (a pool would otherwise check out a different connection,
20	  leaving the lock ineffective);
21	- a **once-per-process-per-conn guard** (``setup()`` is idempotent but re-locking + replaying
22	  the migration query on every call is wasteful), with an in-process lock making the
23	  check-and-set atomic so concurrent threads don't both run setup;
24	- **credential redaction**: a backend exception (psycopg/psycopg_pool) can embed the full
25	  ``user:password`` URI/DSN in its own message, so the conn string is scrubbed precisely and
26	  the exception text via URI-userinfo + DSN-password backstops; the redacted message is then
27	  raised *outside* the exception handler, so the credential-bearing original is retained as
28	  neither ``__cause__`` nor ``__context__`` (a logger walking ``__context__`` cannot
29	  re-expose it);
30	- a single shared **atexit** pool-cleanup handler (one per process, not one per call) so the
31	  pool's worker thread is joined before interpreter finalization.
32	
33	LangGraph/psycopg are optional (the ``persistence`` extra) and imported lazily; the
34	credential-redaction and connection-resolution logic is pure and unit-tested offline.
35	"""
36	
37	from __future__ import annotations
38	
39	import atexit
40	import os
41	import re
42	import threading
43	from typing import Any, cast
44	from urllib.parse import urlsplit
45	
46	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
47	
48	_DEFAULT_PG = "postgresql://localhost:5432/assay"
49	
50	# Arbitrary fixed key for the Postgres session advisory lock that serializes checkpoint
51	# schema migrations across concurrent initializers. The value is irrelevant beyond being a
52	# stable per-logical-schema constant; "ASSY" as an int.
53	_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY = 0x4153_5359
54	
55	# Upper bound (milliseconds) on how long the cross-process advisory lock may be waited for
56	# during schema bootstrap. A stuck/crashed peer that still holds a live PG session on the lock
57	# must NOT be able to hang bootstrap forever (#129); lock_timeout aborts the wait. 0 disables
58	# the bound (block indefinitely, the pre-#129 behavior).
59	_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS = max(0, int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS", "30000")))
60	
61	# Brief meta-lock guarding the process-local registries below (atexit registration, the open-pool
62	# list, the per-conn lock map, the pool cache). It is held ONLY for in-memory check-and-set — it
63	# is NEVER held across blocking cross-process I/O (pool.connection(), the advisory-lock wait, or
64	# setup() DDL). That blocking work is serialized per conn_str instead (#129), so a stuck advisory
65	# lock on one database cannot block initialization of an unrelated one.
66	_init_lock = threading.Lock()
67	
68	# Per-conn_str bootstrap locks: only same-conn_str initializers serialize; different databases
69	# bootstrap concurrently. Created lazily under _init_lock.
70	_CONN_INIT_LOCKS: dict[str, threading.Lock] = {}
71	
72	# Live pools cached by conn_str so the factory reuses one self-healing pool (and its worker
73	# thread) per database rather than opening a fresh pool on every call (#144).
74	_POOLS_BY_CONN: dict[str, Any] = {}
75	
76	# Pools opened by the factory, closed once at process exit via a single shared atexit handler
77	# (registering one handler per call would accumulate handlers across repeated factory calls).
78	_OPEN_POOLS: list[Any] = []
79	_atexit_registered = False
80	
81	# conn_strs whose schema bootstrap (advisory lock + setup() DDL) has already run in this
82	# process — bootstrap once per process per schema; the advisory lock still guards the
83	# first-time cross-process race.
84	_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS: set[str] = set()
85	
86	# Strips ``://[REDACTED]@`` (URI userinfo) from any URI-like substring. '/' is allowed
87	# inside the userinfo so a password containing '/' is still redacted; only '@' (the userinfo
88	# terminator) and whitespace bound the match.
89	_CREDS_RE = re.compile(r"://[REDACTED]@\s]+@")
90	# Strips a libpq keyword/value DSN password token (``password=[REDACTED] / ``pgpassword=...``),
91	# handling quoted values that may contain spaces.
92	_DSN_PW_RE = re.compile(r"(?i)\b(password|pgpassword)\s*=\s*('[^']*'|\"[^\"]*\"|\S+)")
93	
94	
95	def _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str: str) -> str:
96	    """Return ``scheme://host[:port]/path`` with any ``user:password`` userinfo stripped.
97	
98	    Robust to malformed userinfo: a password containing ``/``/``#`` makes ``urlsplit``'s
99	    ``.port`` raise ``ValueError`` on access, so the whole parse (including attribute access)
100	    is guarded and falls back to the generic userinfo-strip regex / a constant placeholder.
101	    """
102	    try:
103	        u = urlsplit(conn_str)
104	        host = u.hostname or ""
105	        port = u.port
106	        scheme = u.scheme
107	        path = u.path
108	    except ValueError:
109	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str)
110	    if not host:
111	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str) if scheme else "<store>"
112	    if ":" in host:  # IPv6 literal — urlsplit drops the brackets; restore them
113	        host = f"[{host}]"
114	    netloc = f"{host}:{port}" if port else host
115	    return f"{scheme}://{netloc}{path}" if scheme else "<store>"
116	
117	
118	def redact_creds(text: str, conn_str: str = "") -> str:
119	    """Remove credentials a backend exception may embed in its message text.
120	
121	    Layers, in order of precision:
122	
123	    1. exact replacement of the known ``conn_str`` (handles any password characters, incl.
124	       ``/`` and spaces) — this is the precise layer and covers the real connection string;
125	    2. a generic URI-userinfo strip (``://[REDACTED]@`` → ``://``) as a backstop for any
126	       *other* URI in the text;
127	    3. a libpq DSN ``password=[REDACTED] strip for the keyword/value form.
128	
129	    Layers 2–3 are best-effort for *secondary* endpoints (e.g. a replica DSN) the caller did
130	    not pass as ``conn_str``; the primary connection string is always scrubbed precisely.
131	    """
132	    if conn_str:
133	        text = text.replace(conn_str, _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str))
134	    text = _CREDS_RE.sub("://", text)
135	    return _DSN_PW_RE.sub(r"\1=***", text)
136	
137	
138	def get_postgres_connection_string() -> str:
139	    """Resolve the Postgres connection string (``ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL`` env → loopback default).
140	
141	    The env path bypasses any external config validation, so loopback is enforced here at the
142	    point of use (ADR-0003 defense-in-depth): graph state must not leave the box.
143	    """
144	    url = os.environ.get("ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL") or _DEFAULT_PG
145	    return require_local_uri(url, what="checkpointer connection")
146	
147	
148	def _safe_close(pool: Any) -> None:
149	    """Close one pool, swallowing errors so a single failing ``close()`` does not abort
150	    cleanup of the remaining pools at interpreter exit (``close()`` calls a C ``finish()``
151	    that can raise)."""
152	    try:
153	        pool.close()
154	    except Exception:
155	        pass
156	
157	
158	def _close_all_pools() -> None:
159	    for p in _OPEN_POOLS:
160	        _safe_close(p)
161	
162	
163	def _register_pool_cleanup(pool: Any) -> None:
164	    global _atexit_registered
165	    with _init_lock:
166	        _OPEN_POOLS.append(pool)
167	        if not _atexit_registered:
168	            atexit.register(_close_all_pools)
169	            _atexit_registered = True
170	
171	
172	def _conn_init_lock(conn_str: str) -> threading.Lock:
173	    """Return the per-conn_str bootstrap lock, creating it under the brief meta-lock (#129)."""
174	    with _init_lock:
175	        lock = _CONN_INIT_LOCKS.get(conn_str)
176	        if lock is None:
177	            lock = _CONN_INIT_LOCKS[conn_str] = threading.Lock()
178	        return lock
179	
180	
181	def _cached_pool(conn_str: str) -> Any:
182	    with _init_lock:
183	        return _POOLS_BY_CONN.get(conn_str)
184	
185	
186	def _acquire_migration_lock(conn: Any) -> None:
187	    """Acquire the cross-process schema-migration advisory lock with a bounded wait (#129).
188	
189	    ``lock_timeout`` aborts the ``pg_advisory_lock`` wait if a peer holds it past the deadline,
190	    so a stuck/crashed initializer cannot hang bootstrap indefinitely; the resulting error
191	    propagates as a redacted ``RuntimeError`` like any other backend failure. set_config is used
192	    (not ``SET``) so the value can be safely parameterized.
193	    """
194	    if _MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS:
195	        conn.execute(
196	            "SELECT set_config('lock_timeout', %s, false)", (str(_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS),)
197	        )
198	    conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_lock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
199	
200	
201	def get_checkpointer(use_memory: bool = False) -> Any:
202	    """Return a configured LangGraph checkpointer.
203	
204	    ``use_memory=True`` returns an in-memory saver (tests / no-Postgres). Otherwise a
205	    Postgres-backed saver over a self-healing pool, with advisory-locked one-time schema
206	    setup. Raises ``RuntimeError`` (with credentials redacted) if the backend is unreachable
207	    or the extra is not installed.
208	    """
209	    try:
210	        if use_memory:
211	            from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
212	
213	            return MemorySaver()
214	        from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
215	        from psycopg.rows import dict_row
216	        from psycopg_pool import ConnectionPool
217	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
218	        raise RuntimeError(
219	            "Postgres checkpointer requires the 'persistence' extra "
220	            "(langgraph-checkpoint-postgres, psycopg[binary,pool]) — not installed"
221	        ) from exc
222	
223	    conn_str = get_postgres_connection_string()
224	
225	    # Fast path: reuse a live, self-healing pool for this conn_str rather than opening a new pool
226	    # (and its background worker thread) on every call (#144). The liveness check on checkout
227	    # replaces dead connections, so a cached pool stays valid across transient outages.
228	    cached = _cached_pool(conn_str)
229	    if cached is not None:
230	        return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, cached))
231	
232	    # Serialize bootstrap PER conn_str — NOT process-globally (#129). Unrelated databases
233	    # initialize concurrently, and a stuck advisory lock on one conn_str can never block init of
234	    # another. The brief meta-lock (_init_lock) is only taken inside the helpers, never held
235	    # across the blocking advisory-lock wait / DDL below.
236	    with _conn_init_lock(conn_str):
237	        cached = _cached_pool(conn_str)  # another thread may have built it while we waited
238	        if cached is not None:
239	            return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, cached))
240	
241	        failure: str | None = None
242	        pool: Any = None
243	        try:
244	            pool = ConnectionPool(
245	                conn_str,
246	                min_size=1,
247	                max_size=8,
248	                kwargs={"autocommit": True, "prepare_threshold": 0, "row_factory": dict_row},
249	                check=ConnectionPool.check_connection,  # liveness check on checkout (self-healing)
250	                open=True,
251	            )
252	            _register_pool_cleanup(pool)
253	            # Bootstrap the schema once per process per conn_str. The advisory lock and the DDL
254	            # must run on the SAME connection (pg_advisory_lock is session-scoped); a pool-backed
255	            # setup() would lock one connection and DDL on another, leaving the lock ineffective.
256	            with pool.connection() as conn:
257	                _acquire_migration_lock(conn)  # bounded wait so a stuck peer can't hang us (#129)
258	                try:
259	                    # row_factory=dict_row makes this connection dict-rowed at runtime
260	                    # (what PostgresSaver requires); the cast asserts that runtime truth
261	                    # the type system can't infer from the pool kwargs.
262	                    PostgresSaver(cast(Any, conn)).setup()  # DDL on the locked connection
263	                finally:
264	                    # Best-effort unlock; the lock auto-releases when the connection
265	                    # returns to the pool, and raising here would mask a setup() error.
266	                    try:
267	                        conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
268	                    except Exception:
269	                        pass
270	            with _init_lock:
271	                _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS.add(conn_str)
272	                _POOLS_BY_CONN[conn_str] = pool  # cache for reuse on later calls (#144)
273	            return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, pool))  # dict-rowed pool (row_factory=dict_row)
274	        except Exception as exc:
275	            # Build the redacted message here, but raise it OUTSIDE the handler (below) so the
276	            # original — credential-bearing — exception is retained as neither __cause__ NOR
277	            # __context__ (a logger walking __context__ would otherwise re-expose the password).
278	            failure = (
279	                f"Postgres checkpointer connection failed (is Postgres running on "
280	                f"{_sanitize_conn_str(conn_str)}?): {redact_creds(str(exc), conn_str)}. "
281	                "For tests without Postgres, use get_checkpointer(use_memory=True)."
282	            )
283	            # The pool may have opened before bootstrap failed — close it and drop it from the
284	            # cleanup registry so a failed init does not leak the connection/worker thread (#105).
285	            if pool is not None:
286	                _safe_close(pool)
287	                with _init_lock:
288	                    if pool in _OPEN_POOLS:
289	                        _OPEN_POOLS.remove(pool)
290	        raise RuntimeError(failure)
291	
292	
293	def configured_checkpointer(use_memory: bool | None = None) -> Any:
294	    """Return the checkpointer the environment selects.
295	
296	    ``use_memory`` (or ``ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND=memory``) chooses the in-memory saver;
297	    otherwise Postgres.
298	    """
299	    if use_memory is None:
300	        use_memory = os.environ.get("ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND", "postgres").lower() == "memory"
301	    return get_checkpointer(use_memory=use_memory)
302

1	"""Local vector store seam (ADR-0003) — loopback-enforced Qdrant factory.
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform. The client is constructed only against a
4	loopback host; the guard fires *before* importing the (optional ``persistence`` extra)
5	``qdrant_client``, so a misconfigured remote host is rejected regardless of whether the
6	package is installed.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import os
12	import uuid
13	from typing import Any, Protocol, Sequence
14	
15	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_host
16	
17	VECTOR_HOST = os.environ.get("ASSAY_VECTOR_HOST", "localhost")
18	VECTOR_HTTP_PORT = int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_VECTOR_HTTP_PORT", "6333"))
19	
20	# Stable namespace for mapping the engine's string ids onto Qdrant point UUIDs.
21	_POINT_NS = uuid.UUID("a55a9e00-0000-4000-8000-000000000001")
22	
23	
24	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
25	class VectorStore(Protocol):
26	    def upsert(self, ids: Sequence[str], vectors: Sequence[Sequence[float]]) -> None: ...
27	    def query(self, vector: Sequence[float], k: int) -> list[tuple[str, float]]: ...
28	
29	
30	def vector_store_url() -> str:
31	    host = require_loopback_host(VECTOR_HOST, what="vector store host")
32	    return f"http://{host}:{VECTOR_HTTP_PORT}"
33	
34	
35	def get_qdrant_client() -> Any:
36	    """Construct a loopback-only Qdrant client (lazy import of the optional extra)."""
37	    host = require_loopback_host(VECTOR_HOST, what="vector store host")  # before any import
38	    try:
39	        from qdrant_client import QdrantClient
40	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
41	        raise RuntimeError(
42	            "vector store requires the 'persistence' extra (qdrant-client) — not installed"
43	        ) from exc
44	    return QdrantClient(host=host, port=VECTOR_HTTP_PORT)
45	
46	
47	def _point_id(ref: str) -> str:
48	    """Deterministic Qdrant point UUID for an engine string id (Qdrant ids must be int/UUID)."""
49	    return str(uuid.uuid5(_POINT_NS, ref))
50	
51	
52	class QdrantVectorStore:
53	    """Reference :class:`VectorStore` over a loopback Qdrant collection (cosine).
54	
55	    The engine's ids are arbitrary strings; Qdrant point ids must be int/UUID, so each id is
56	    mapped to a deterministic UUID and the original id is stored in the point payload (and
57	    returned by ``query``). This is a goal-agnostic reference implementation; a study may
58	    supply its own ``VectorStore`` (collection naming, distance, sharding are study choices).
59	    """
60	
61	    def __init__(self, collection: str, dim: int, *, client: Any | None = None) -> None:
62	        self._collection = collection
63	        self._dim = dim
64	        # Track ownership: only close a client we created ourselves; an injected client is the
65	        # caller's to manage (#117).
66	        self._owns_client = client is None
67	        self._client = client if client is not None else get_qdrant_client()
68	
69	    def close(self) -> None:
70	        """Close the underlying client if this store created it (no-op for an injected one)."""
71	        if self._owns_client:
72	            close = getattr(self._client, "close", None)
73	            if callable(close):
74	                close()
75	
76	    def __enter__(self) -> "QdrantVectorStore":
77	        return self
78	
79	    def __exit__(self, *_exc: object) -> None:
80	        self.close()
81	
82	    def ensure_collection(self) -> None:
83	        from qdrant_client.models import Distance, VectorParams
84	
85	        if not self._client.collection_exists(self._collection):
86	            self._client.create_collection(
87	                self._collection,
88	                vectors_config=VectorParams(size=self._dim, distance=Distance.COSINE),
89	            )
90	
91	    def upsert(
92	        self, ids: Sequence[str], vectors: Sequence[Sequence[float]], *, batch_size: int = 256
93	    ) -> None:
94	        from qdrant_client.models import PointStruct
95	
96	        if len(ids) != len(vectors):
97	            raise ValueError("ids and vectors must have equal length")
98	        if batch_size < 1:
99	            raise ValueError("batch_size must be >= 1")
100	        # Build each batch of PointStructs lazily, so only one batch is materialized in memory at
101	        # a time (bounding BOTH payload AND memory) rather than boxing the whole corpus up front
102	        # (#118). Qdrant guidance: don't upload point-by-point, but bound the batch.
103	        for start in range(0, len(ids), batch_size):
104	            batch = [
105	                PointStruct(id=_point_id(ids[j]), vector=list(vectors[j]), payload={"ref": ids[j]})
106	                for j in range(start, min(start + batch_size, len(ids)))
107	            ]
108	            self._client.upsert(self._collection, points=batch)
109	
110	    def query(self, vector: Sequence[float], k: int) -> list[tuple[str, float]]:
111	        res = self._client.query_points(
112	            self._collection, query=list(vector), limit=k, with_payload=True
113	        )
114	        return [(p.payload["ref"], float(p.score)) for p in res.points]
115

1	"""Append-only provenance trail (GOVERNANCE §3, W3C PROV-DM style).
2	
3	Every action a study takes is recorded, in order, before the next executes: ingestion, the
4	blind baseline build, each discovered/locked hypothesis, every gate decision, each verdict,
5	the source scorecard, the final report. The trail is the spine of reproducibility and of the
6	independence claim — "no step can be retroactively removed or reordered" (GOVERNANCE §3).
7	
8	The trail enforces that structurally, not by promise:
9	
10	- **Append-only.** Entries are added through :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.record` only; there is no
11	  remove/edit/reorder API and the exposed view is an immutable tuple of frozen entries.
12	- **Chained.** Each entry carries ``prev_hash`` and an ``entry_hash`` over
13	  ``(prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp)`` using the engine's one type-faithful
14	  serializer (:mod:`assay_engine._canonical`), rooted at a fixed genesis.
15	
16	Integrity — honest scope, two tiers:
17	
18	- **Unkeyed (default).** The chain is plain SHA-256. It is *tamper-evident against naive
19	  tampering and accidental corruption*: editing one entry without recomputing downstream
20	  hashes, reordering, or deleting an entry is caught by :func:`verify_records`. It is **not**
21	  forgery-proof: a motivated party who controls the serialized bytes can edit a payload and
22	  recompute the whole genesis-rooted chain, and the result verifies. Unkeyed integrity buys
23	  "is this a valid chain?", not "is this *the* chain the engine produced."
24	- **Keyed (``secret=...``).** Pass an on-box secret (as pre-registration does, ADR-0009) and
25	  the chain is HMAC-SHA256. The head cannot be recomputed without the secret, so a downstream
26	  store cannot silently rewrite history and re-seal it; :func:`verify_records` must be given
27	  the same secret. This is local tamper-*resistance*; non-repudiable third-party attestation of
28	  the trail's time remains the same pluggable concern as pre-registration, out of scope here.
29	"""
30	
31	from __future__ import annotations
32	
33	import datetime as _dt
34	import hashlib
35	import hmac
36	import threading
37	from dataclasses import dataclass
38	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping
39	
40	from assay_engine._canonical import canonical_json
41	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
42	
43	_UTC = _dt.timezone.utc
44	_GENESIS = "assay-provenance:v1:genesis"
45	
46	Clock = Callable[[], _dt.datetime]
47	
48	
49	class ProvenanceError(RuntimeError):
50	    """The provenance trail is inconsistent (a broken chain) or an entry cannot be recorded."""
51	
52	
53	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
54	class ProvenanceEntry:
55	    """One immutable, chained record of a single action."""
56	
57	    seq: int
58	    kind: str
59	    summary: str
60	    payload: Mapping[str, Any]
61	    timestamp: str  # ISO-8601 UTC
62	    prev_hash: str
63	    entry_hash: str
64	
65	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
66	        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", freeze_mapping(self.payload))
67	
68	
69	def _digest(
70	    seq: int,
71	    kind: str,
72	    summary: str,
73	    payload: Mapping[str, Any],
74	    timestamp: str,
75	    prev_hash: str,
76	    secret: bytes | None,
77	) -> str:
78	    try:
79	        msg = canonical_json(
80	            ["assay-prov:v1", prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, dict(payload), timestamp]
81	        ).encode("utf-8")
82	    except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
83	        # RecursionError (deeply nested payload) is a RuntimeError, not Value/TypeError — catch it
84	        # too so the entrypoints surface a typed ProvenanceError, never a raw exception (#89).
85	        raise ProvenanceError(
86	            f"provenance payload for {kind!r} cannot be serialized reproducibly ({exc}); "
87	            "record only JSON-native / hashable values of bounded depth"
88	        ) from None
89	    if secret is None:
90	        return hashlib.sha256(msg).hexdigest()
91	    return hmac.new(secret, msg, "sha256").hexdigest()
92	
93	
94	def _utc_now() -> _dt.datetime:
95	    return _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
96	
97	
98	class ProvenanceTrail:
99	    """An append-only, chained audit trail for one study run.
100	
101	    ``secret`` (>= 16 bytes) keys the chain with HMAC so a serialized trail cannot be forged
102	    without it (see module docstring). ``clock`` injects the timestamp source (deterministic
103	    tests); it must return a timezone-aware ``datetime``.
104	    """
105	
106	    def __init__(self, *, secret: bytes | None = None, clock: Clock | None = None) -> None:
107	        if secret is not None and (not isinstance(secret, (bytes, bytearray)) or len(secret) < 16):
108	            raise ValueError("provenance secret must be >= 16 bytes of key material")
109	        self._entries: list[ProvenanceEntry] = []
110	        self._secret: bytes | None = bytes(secret) if secret is not None else None
111	        self._clock: Clock = clock or _utc_now
112	        # serialize the read-compute-append so concurrent record() calls cannot interleave and
113	        # corrupt the seq/prev_hash chain (#114)
114	        self._lock = threading.Lock()
115	
116	    def record(self, kind: str, summary: str, **payload: Any) -> ProvenanceEntry:
117	        """Append one entry. Returns it. The only way to add to the trail. Thread-safe."""
118	        if not kind:
119	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance entry kind must be non-empty")
120	        with self._lock:
121	            return self._record_locked(kind, summary, payload)
122	
123	    def _record_locked(
124	        self, kind: str, summary: str, payload: Mapping[str, Any]
125	    ) -> ProvenanceEntry:
126	        seq = len(self._entries)
127	        prev_hash = self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
128	        try:
129	            instant = self._clock()
130	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the clock is an injected dependency
131	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance clock raised: {exc}") from None
132	        if not isinstance(instant, _dt.datetime):
133	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a datetime")
134	        try:
135	            # utcoffset()/astimezone()/isoformat() can raise on a hostile datetime subclass or an
136	            # out-of-range value — keep the typed-error contract (#96).
137	            if instant.tzinfo is None or instant.utcoffset() is None:
138	                raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a timezone-aware datetime")
139	            timestamp = instant.astimezone(_UTC).isoformat()
140	        except ProvenanceError:
141	            raise
142	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
143	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance timestamp could not be normalized: {exc}") from None
144	        entry_hash = _digest(seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp, prev_hash, self._secret)
145	        try:
146	            entry = ProvenanceEntry(
147	                seq=seq,
148	                kind=kind,
149	                summary=summary,
150	                payload=payload,
151	                timestamp=timestamp,
152	                prev_hash=prev_hash,
153	                entry_hash=entry_hash,
154	            )
155	        except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
156	            raise ProvenanceError(
157	                f"provenance entry for {kind!r} could not be frozen ({exc})"
158	            ) from None
159	        self._entries.append(entry)
160	        return entry
161	
162	    def as_recorder(self) -> Callable[[Any], None]:
163	        """A :data:`gates.ProvenanceRecorder` bound to this trail (records GateDecisions).
164	
165	        Reads each attribute exactly once and defensively, so a booby-trapped decision object
166	        (a property that raises) surfaces a typed ``ProvenanceError`` rather than a raw error.
167	        """
168	
169	        def _record(decision: Any) -> None:
170	            try:

    tracer_provider_kwargs = kwargs.copy()

    if "resource" not in tracer_provider_kwargs:
        # No resource provided, create one with project name
        tracer_provider_kwargs["resource"] = Resource.create({PROJECT_NAME: project_name})
    else:
        # Resource provided, merge project name into it
        existing_resource = tracer_provider_kwargs["resource"]
        # Create project resource without default attributes to avoid overriding user attributes
        project_attributes = {PROJECT_NAME: project_name}
        project_resource = Resource(attributes=project_attributes)
        tracer_provider_kwargs["resource"] = existing_resource.merge(project_resource)

    # Ensure TracerProvider verbose is False (register handles its own verbose output)
    tracer_provider_kwargs["verbose"] = False

    # Handle API key by adding to headers
    if api_key:
        if headers is None:
            headers = {}
        else:
            headers = headers.copy()  # Don't modify the original dict
        headers["authorization"] = f"Bearer {api_key}"

    tracer_provider = TracerProvider(protocol=protocol, **tracer_provider_kwargs)
    span_processor: SpanProcessor
    if batch:
